title: rhododendron molle subsp. japonicum
intro: Rhododendron molle subsp. japonicum, also known as Japanese dwarf azalea, is a subspecies of the Rhododendron molle plant, which is a type of evergreen shrub native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rhododendron molle subsp. japonicum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
